channel.title: Fitz HR — HR & Award Blog
channel.description: Award rates, Fair Work compliance and practical HR guidance for Australian employers — grounded in your modern award, not a generic template.

item.title: Children's Services Broken Shift 2026: Rules (MA000120)
item.description: Children's Services Award MA000120 broken shift allowance — the $21.38 per day rate, common in OSHC, and how minimum engagement applies.

item.title: Children's Services Casual Conversion 2026: The Rules
item.description: Children's Services Award MA000120 casual conversion — the employee-initiated rules since 26 Feb 2025, eligibility, the 21-day response and what changes.

item.title: Educational Leader Allowance 2026: Rules (MA000120)
item.description: Children's Services Award MA000120 educational leader allowance — the $4,784.28 per year rate, when it applies and how it is pro-rated.

item.title: Children's Services Penalty Rates 2026 (MA000120)
item.description: Children's Services Award MA000120 penalty rates — Saturday, Sunday and public holiday rates for full-time, part-time & casual staff, plus shift loadings.

item.title: Children's Services Award Rates 2026: Pay (MA000120)
item.description: Children's Services Award MA000120 pay rates 2026 — educator and director rates, weekend penalties, shift loadings and the educational leader allowance.

item.title: Health Award Allowances 2026: Uniform, Tools & More
item.description: Health Professionals Award MA000027 allowances 2026 — uniform, tool and other common allowances, what triggers them and the current amounts.

item.title: Health Award Annualised Wages 2026: Clause 22 Rules
item.description: Annualised wage arrangements under the Health Professionals Award MA000027 — eligibility, outer-limit hours, the annual reconciliation and record-keeping.

item.title: Health Award Casual Conversion 2026: The Rules
item.description: Health Professionals Award MA000027 casual conversion — the employee-initiated rules since 26 Feb 2025, eligibility, the 21-day response and what changes.

item.title: Health Professionals Award Rates 2026: Pay (MA000027)
item.description: Health Professionals Award MA000027 pay rates 2026 — stream and level rates, weekend penalties, shift and casual loading and minimum engagement.

item.title: Health Award Penalty Rates 2026: Weekend & Public Holiday
item.description: Health Professionals Award MA000027 penalty rates — Saturday, Sunday and public holiday rates for full-time, part-time and casual staff, plus overtime.

item.title: Manufacturing Award Allowances 2026: Tool, Meal & More
item.description: Manufacturing Award MA000010 allowances 2026 — the tool allowance, meal allowance and other common allowances, what triggers them, and the current amounts.

item.title: Manufacturing Award Casual Conversion 2026: The Rules
item.description: Manufacturing Award MA000010 casual conversion — the employee-initiated rules since 26 Feb 2025, eligibility, the 21-day response and what changes.

item.title: Manufacturing Award Overtime 2026: Rates & Rules (MA000010)
item.description: Manufacturing Award MA000010 overtime 2026 — 150% for the first 3 hours then 200%, Sunday overtime, the overtime triggers and rest-break rules.

item.title: Manufacturing Award Rates 2026: C-Level Pay (MA000010)
item.description: Manufacturing Award MA000010 pay rates 2026 — the C14–C-level structure, hourly rates, weekend and shift penalties, overtime and casual loading.

item.title: Manufacturing Award Shift Loadings 2026: Afternoon & Night
item.description: Manufacturing Award MA000010 shift loadings — the 15% afternoon and night loading, the 30% permanent night rate, and how they interact with penalties.

item.title: Retail Award Casual Conversion 2026: Employee-Initiated Rules
item.description: Retail Award MA000004 casual conversion — the employee-initiated rules since 26 Feb 2025, eligibility, the 21-day response and what changes.

item.title: Retail Award Evening Loading 2026: The 25% After 6pm Rule
item.description: How the Retail Award MA000004 evening loading works — the 25% on ordinary hours after 6pm Mon–Fri, when it applies, and the common mistakes.

item.title: Retail Award Minimum Engagement 2026: The 3-Hour Rule
item.description: Retail Award MA000004 minimum engagement — part-time and casual retail staff must be paid for at least 3 hours per shift. Rostering rules explained.

item.title: Retail Award Penalty Rates 2026: Sunday & Public Holiday
item.description: Retail Award MA000004 penalty rates 2026 — Saturday, Sunday and public holiday rates for full-time, part-time and casual staff, with worked examples.

item.title: Retail Award Rates 2026: Pay & Penalty Rates (MA000004)
item.description: Retail Award MA000004 pay rates 2026 — Retail Employee Level 1–8 hourly rates, weekend and public holiday penalties, casual loading and evening loading.

item.title: SCHADS Broken Shift Allowance 2026: Rules (MA000100)
item.description: SCHADS Award MA000100 broken shifts 2026 — the $21.81 / $28.87 broken shift allowance, the maximum unpaid breaks, and the minimum engagement rules.

item.title: SCHADS Casual Conversion 2026: The Rules (MA000100)
item.description: SCHADS Award MA000100 casual conversion — the employee-initiated rules since 26 Feb 2025, eligibility, the 21-day response and what changes.

item.title: SCHADS Penalty Rates 2026: Weekend & Public Holiday
item.description: SCHADS Award MA000100 penalty rates — Saturday, Sunday and public holiday rates for full-time, part-time and casual staff, plus shift loadings.

item.title: SCHADS Award Rates 2026: Pay & Penalty Rates (MA000100)
item.description: SCHADS Award MA000100 pay rates 2026 — social, community and home care rates, weekend and public holiday penalties, casual and shift loadings.

item.title: SCHADS Sleepover Allowance 2026: Rules & Rate (MA000100)
item.description: The SCHADS Award MA000100 sleepover allowance 2026 — the $62.87 per night rate, what counts as a sleepover, and how active work during a sleepover is paid.

item.title: Children's Services Award Wage Rise 2026: 4.75% (MA000120)
item.description: Fair Work lifts MA000120 award minimums 4.75% from 1 July 2026 — what it means for children's services employers, new rates and a pre-1-July checklist.

item.title: Health Professionals Award Wage Rise 2026: 4.75% (MA000027)
item.description: Fair Work lifts MA000027 award minimums 4.75% from 1 July 2026 — what it means for health practice employers, new rates and a pre-1-July checklist.

item.title: Manufacturing Award Wage Rise 2026: 4.75% (MA000010)
item.description: Fair Work lifts MA000010 award minimums 4.75% from 1 July 2026 — what it means for manufacturing employers, new rates and a pre-1-July checklist.

item.title: Minimum Wage Increase 2026: 4.75% Pay Rise
item.description: Fair Work's 2026 wage review lifts award minimums 4.75% from 1 July 2026 — what it means for hospitality & restaurant employers, plus a pre-1-July checklist.

item.title: Retail Award Wage Rise 2026: 4.75% (MA000004)
item.description: Fair Work lifts MA000004 award minimums 4.75% from 1 July 2026 — what it means for retail employers, new rates and a pre-1-July checklist.

item.title: SCHADS Award Wage Rise 2026: 4.75% (MA000100)
item.description: Fair Work lifts MA000100 award minimums 4.75% from 1 July 2026 — what it means for community services employers, new rates and a pre-1-July checklist.

item.title: Annual Leave Loading & Cashing Out (MA000009/MA000119)
item.description: The 17.5% annual leave loading under MA000009 and MA000119, plus NES cashing-out rules — eligibility, the 4-week balance rule, and termination treatment.

item.title: Annualised Wage Arrangements (MA000119 cl. 20)
item.description: MA000119 annualised wage explained — Clause 20, the 25% loading, the 18/12 outer limits, the 12-month reconciliation, and record-keeping rules in 2026.

item.title: How to Calculate Back-Pay for Underpaid Staff
item.description: Step-by-step guide to calculating back-pay for underpaid hospitality staff — Award gap analysis, 6-year limitation, interest, super, and FWO remediation.

item.title: Probation Periods in Hospitality Australia
item.description: Probation periods in Australian hospitality — contractual probation vs the Minimum Employment Period (s383), and the protections that still apply.

item.title: Public Holiday Pay — Hospitality Award (MA000009)
item.description: MA000009 public holiday pay 2026 — 225% and 250% rates, the substitute day option, right-to-refuse, and penalty calculations for pubs and hotels.

item.title: Public Holiday Pay — Restaurant Award (MA000119)
item.description: MA000119 public holiday penalty rates 2026 — 225% and 250% rates, the substitute day option, Christmas-on-weekend rule, and 4-hour minimum engagement.

item.title: Stand-Down vs Termination (Fair Work Act s524)
item.description: Stand-down vs termination under the Fair Work Act — when s524 applies, the 3 lawful grounds, why a downturn isn't enough, and how it differs from redundancy.

item.title: Hospitality vs Restaurant Award AU: Key Differences
item.description: Compare the Hospitality Award (MA000009) and Restaurant Award (MA000119) in Australia. Coverage, penalty rates, minimum engagements & which Award applies.

item.title: Last-Minute Roster Changes (MA000119)
item.description: Restaurant Award MA000119 roster change rules — the 7-day notice requirement, mutual agreement, RDO changes, and the consultation obligation in 2026.

item.title: Casual Conversion Rules — Restaurant Award (MA000119) 2026
item.description: MA000119 casual conversion rules 2026 — employee-initiated framework, 6-month threshold, employer response obligations, and CEIS.

item.title: Restaurant Award Break Entitlements (MA000119)
item.description: MA000119 break entitlements 2026 — meal breaks, rest breaks, delayed meal break penalty, and shift-length thresholds for cafes and restaurants.

item.title: Restaurant Award Pay Rates 2026 (MA000119)
item.description: MA000119 pay rates 2026 — adult, junior, casual, and apprentice rates by classification level, plus penalties, loadings, and overtime.

item.title: Split Shift Rules — Restaurant Award (MA000119)
item.description: Restaurant Award MA000119 split shift allowance, maximum spread of hours, and minimum engagement rules for full-time, part-time, and casual employees in 2026.

item.title: Restaurant Staff Underpayment Penalties 2026
item.description: Restaurant underpayment 2026 — civil penalties up to $4.95M, criminal wage theft jail terms, and director liability under the Fair Work Act.

item.title: How to Handle a Staff Complaint in Hospitality
item.description: A staff complaint — whether about harassment, unfair treatment, discrimination, or workplace conflict — is one of the most legally sensiti

item.title: What Is Serious Misconduct in Australia?
item.description: Serious misconduct is the only basis in Australian employment law that allows immediate dismissal without prior warnings. But “serious misconduc

item.title: Can I Stop Rostering a Casual Employee in Australia?
item.description: Can you just stop rostering a casual employee in Australia? The answer depends on how long they've worked and their roster pattern.

item.title: Fair Work Fines for Hospitality Australia
item.description: Real Fair Work penalty figures for Australian hospitality venues. Covers underpayment fines, record-keeping penalties, and wage theft criminal liability.

item.title: Can I Fire Someone During Probation in Australia? (2026)
item.description: What probation actually means in Australian employment law, when you can terminate during probation, and how to protect your venue.

item.title: How Long Does a Written Warning Last in Australia? (2026)
item.description: How long does a formal warning stay active in Australian employment law? What the Fair Work Commission actually looks for when assessing warning sequences.

item.title: PIP Template for Australian Hospitality (2026)
item.description: Free performance improvement plan template for Australian hospitality venues. Covers required elements, timeframes, and how a PIP protects you at Fair Work.

item.title: How Much Does an Unfair Dismissal Claim Cost in Australia?
item.description: Real numbers on unfair dismissal costs for Australian hospitality. Compensation caps, legal fees, settlement figures, and how to avoid a claim.

item.title: Can I Change a Roster Last Minute in Australia?
item.description: Can you change a roster last minute in Australia or must you give notice? Find out what the Hospitality Award says, when it's lawful, and what you must pay.

item.title: Can I Fire Someone on the Spot in Australia?
item.description: Can you instantly dismiss an employee in Australia? When summary dismissal is lawful, when it creates a claim, and what to do to protect your venue.

item.title: Do I Need to Warn Before Firing? Australia
item.description: Do you need to warn an employee before firing them in Australia? What Fair Work actually requires — and when exceptions apply.

item.title: What Happens if I Underpay Staff in Australia?
item.description: What happens if you underpay staff in Australia — and how serious is it? Back-pay, fines, criminal charges. Here's what hospitality venue owners need to know.

item.title: Employee Threatening to Go to Fair Work — What to Do
item.description: An employee is threatening to go to Fair Work Australia. Don't panic. Here's exactly what to do, what not to say, and how to protect your venue right now.

item.title: Split Shift Rules — Hospitality Award Australia
item.description: Complete guide to split shift rules under the Hospitality Award. Covers allowances, spread of hours, minimum engagement, and common mistakes venues make.

item.title: Workplace Bullying in Hospitality: What to Do
item.description: How to respond to workplace bullying complaints in hospitality. Covers Fair Work obligations, WHS laws, investigation steps, documentation, and penalties.

item.title: Procedural Fairness in Australian Employment Law (2026)
item.description: Plain-English guide to procedural fairness for hospitality managers. What it means, why it matters for unfair dismissal, and how to follow the correct process.

item.title: Break Entitlements Under the Hospitality Award by Shift Length
item.description: Exactly when breaks are required under the Hospitality Award — by shift length. Paid vs unpaid, timing rules, and what happens if you get it wrong.

item.title: Can You Fire a Casual Employee in Australia? (2026 Guide)
item.description: How to legally terminate casual employees in Australian hospitality. Covers unfair dismissal protections, notice requirements and documentation needed.

item.title: Employee No-Shows in Hospitality: What to Do (2026)
item.description: How to handle employee no-shows in hospitality legally. Documentation, communication steps, formal warnings and when you can terminate.

item.title: Casual Conversion Rules — Hospitality Award (MA000009) 2026
item.description: MA000009 casual conversion rules 2026 — employee-initiated framework, 6-month threshold, 21-day employer response, and CEIS obligations.

item.title: Warning Letter for Hospitality Staff (2026)
item.description: How to write a legally compliant formal warning letter for hospitality staff under the Fair Work Act. What to include, common mistakes, and a free template.

item.title: Best HR Software for Hospitality Australia (2026)
item.description: Comparing HR software for Australian pubs, restaurants, cafes and hotels. Features, pricing, and which tools understand the Hospitality Award and Fair Work.

item.title: Employment Contracts for Restaurant Staff
item.description: How to write compliant employment contracts for hospitality staff in Australia. Covers Fair Work requirements, Hospitality Award clauses, and mistakes to avoid.

item.title: Fair Work Compliance Checklist for Hospitality
item.description: Complete Fair Work compliance checklist for Australian pubs, restaurants and hotels. Covers pay rates, record keeping, leave entitlements, and termination.

item.title: Hospitality Award Rates 2026: Penalty Rates Guide
item.description: MA000009 penalty rates 2026 — Saturday, Sunday, public holiday, casual loading, evening/night loadings, and overtime for hospitality staff.

item.title: Managing Underperformance in Hospitality Australia (2026)
item.description: Step-by-step guide for pub, restaurant and hotel managers on handling underperforming staff legally. Covers warnings, PIPs, and termination under Fair Work.
